message:
fix(renderer): correct cursor positioning when continuation cells are skipped

BUG: When rendering dirty regions, the cursor position drifted when
continuation cells (part of wide characters like emoji/CJK) were
skipped. The AnsiWriter's internal cursor tracking assumed continuous
writes, but skipping cells caused subsequent characters to be written
at wrong positions.

SYMPTOM: As log lines scrolled at the bottom of the screen, text
elsewhere became garbled - "HashMap" became "skseshap", "Write"
became "Wslte", etc.

FIX: Move cursor to exact (x, y) position before each cell write,
rather than relying on sequential cursor advancement. This is slightly
less efficient (more cursor move sequences) but guarantees correct
positioning regardless of continuation cells in the dirty region.

The AnsiWriter already optimizes cursor moves (relative vs absolute),
so the overhead is minimal for consecutive cells.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
